title: nogra grahamii
intro: Nogra grahamii, also known as the Philippine oak or Philippine chestnut, is a plant species belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic to help reduce water retention. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion for its medicinal uses, and the leaves and bark can also be used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions or infusions.
